subject: general-knowledge
description: Continents MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: How many continents are there in the world?
mcqs.explanation: There are 7 continents: Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe and Australia/Oceania.

mcqs.q: Which is the largest continent in the world by area?
mcqs.explanation: Asia covers ~43,998,000 km² — about 29.5% of Earth's land area — making it the largest continent.

mcqs.q: Which is the smallest continent by area?
mcqs.explanation: Australia, at ~7,699,000 km² (about 5.2% of Earth's land), is the smallest continent.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous continent?
mcqs.explanation: Asia hosts ~4.83 billion people — about 58.74% of the world's population — by far the most populous.

mcqs.q: Which is the least populated continent (excluding research stations)?
mcqs.explanation: Antarctica has only 3,000–5,000 temporary residents (scientists/researchers) at any time — by far the least populated.

mcqs.q: Which continent covers about 29.5% of Earth's land area?
mcqs.explanation: Asia, at 29.5% of Earth's land area, is by far the largest continent.

mcqs.q: What percentage of Earth's land area is covered by Africa?
mcqs.explanation: Africa is the second-largest continent at about 20% of Earth's land area (~29.8 million km²).

mcqs.q: Which is the second-largest continent by area?
mcqs.explanation: Africa is the second-largest continent at ~29.8 million km².

mcqs.q: Approximately how much of Earth's land area does Antarctica cover?
mcqs.explanation: Antarctica covers about 9.6% of Earth's land area (~13.6 million km²) — the fifth-largest continent.

mcqs.q: Which continent has the highest population density?
mcqs.explanation: Asia has the highest population density at ~156 inhabitants per km².

mcqs.q: Which is the highest point on Earth?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Everest in the Himalayas (Asia) is the highest point on Earth at 8,848.86 m (2020 China-Nepal survey).

mcqs.q: What is the official height of Mount Everest as per the 2020 China-Nepal survey?
mcqs.explanation: The 2020 joint China-Nepal survey set Everest's official height at 8,848.86 m (29,031.7 ft) — an upward revision from the older 8,850 m figure.

mcqs.q: Which is the lowest point on Earth's land surface?
mcqs.explanation: The Dead Sea shore (between Jordan and Israel) at −396.8 m is the lowest land point on Earth.

mcqs.q: What is the highest peak in Africa?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro (5,895 m) in Tanzania is the highest point in Africa and the world's tallest free-standing mountain.

mcqs.q: What is the lowest point in Africa?
mcqs.explanation: Lake Assal in Djibouti at −156.1 m is the lowest point in Africa and the third-lowest land point on Earth.

mcqs.q: What is the highest peak in North America?
mcqs.explanation: Denali (formerly Mount McKinley) in Alaska at 6,190 m is the highest peak in North America. Its name was changed back to McKinley in 2025.

mcqs.q: What is the lowest point in North America?
mcqs.explanation: Death Valley (Badwater Basin) in California at −85.9 m is the lowest point in North America.

mcqs.q: What is the highest peak in South America?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Aconcagua (6,960 m) in Argentina is the highest peak in South America — and the highest outside Asia.

mcqs.q: What is the lowest point in South America?
mcqs.explanation: Laguna del Carbón on the Valdés Peninsula (Argentina) at −39.9 m is the lowest point in South America.

mcqs.q: What is the highest peak in Europe?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Elbrus (5,642 m) in the Caucasus Mountains of Russia is the highest peak in Europe. Mont Blanc (4,810 m) is the highest in Western Europe.

mcqs.q: What is the lowest point in Europe?
mcqs.explanation: The Caspian Sea at −28 m is the lowest point in Europe.

mcqs.q: What is the highest point on mainland Australia?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Kosciuszko (2,228 m) in New South Wales is the highest peak on mainland Australia.

mcqs.q: What is the highest peak in Antarctica?
mcqs.explanation: Vinson Massif (4,897 m) in the Ellsworth Mountains is the highest peak in Antarctica — one of the Seven Summits.

mcqs.q: What is the deepest point in Antarctica (below sea level on land)?
mcqs.explanation: The Bentley Subglacial Trench at −2,555 m is the lowest point on Earth's land surface (covered by ice).

mcqs.q: Who is known as the Father of Geography?
mcqs.explanation: Eratosthenes (276–194 BCE), a Greek mathematician and astronomer, is known as the Father of Geography — he first used the term 'geography' and calculated Earth's circumference.

mcqs.q: Who created the first known world map?
mcqs.explanation: Anaximander, an ancient Greek philosopher, created the first known world map in the 6th century BCE.

mcqs.q: Who is regarded as the Father of Modern Geography?
mcqs.explanation: Alexander von Humboldt (Prussian polymath) is regarded as the Father of Modern Geography. Carl Ritter is also a co-founder.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Human Geography?
mcqs.explanation: French geographer Paul Vidal de la Blache is regarded as the Father of Human Geography.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Environmental Geography?
mcqs.explanation: Alexander von Humboldt is also regarded as the Father of Environmental Geography for his pioneering work on biogeography and ecological zones.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Cultural Geography?
mcqs.explanation: American geographer Carl O. Sauer is regarded as the Father of Cultural Geography for his landscape-and-culture approach.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Political Geography?
mcqs.explanation: German geographer and anthropologist Friedrich Ratzel is regarded as the Father of Political Geography.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Zoogeography?
mcqs.explanation: Alfred Russel Wallace, co-discoverer of natural selection, is regarded as the Father of Zoogeography for his classification of animal regions.

mcqs.q: Who is the Father of Economic Geography?
mcqs.explanation: Scottish geographer George Chisholm is regarded as the Father of Economic Geography. His 1889 'Handbook of Commercial Geography' was foundational.

mcqs.q: What was the world population as of December 2025 (UN data)?
mcqs.explanation: Per the UN, world population reached approximately 8.27 billion in December 2025 — 50.4% male, 49.6% female.

mcqs.q: In which year did India overtake China to become the world's most populous country?
mcqs.explanation: India officially overtook China as the world's most populous country in 2023, per UN data.

mcqs.q: Approximately what percentage of the world's population does India hold?
mcqs.explanation: India has approximately 17.8% of the world's population — the highest of any single country.

mcqs.q: Approximately what percentage of the world's population does China hold?
mcqs.explanation: China has approximately 17.2% of the world's population (just below India since 2023).

mcqs.q: What is Pakistan's approximate share of world population?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan holds approximately 3.22% of the world's population, the 5th-largest country by population.

mcqs.q: Together, China and India hold approximately what share of world population?
mcqs.explanation: China (17.2%) plus India (17.8%) together account for approximately 35% of the world's total population.

mcqs.q: Asia hosts approximately what share of world population?
mcqs.explanation: Asia hosts approximately 58.74% of the world's total population — about 4.83 billion people.

mcqs.q: What is the world population growth rate (2025)?
mcqs.explanation: The world population is growing at approximately 0.85% per year in 2025 — down from 0.97% in 2020 and 1.25% in 2015.

mcqs.q: Approximately how many people are added to the world population annually?
mcqs.explanation: The world population is currently increasing by approximately 70 million people per year.

mcqs.q: According to recent UN data, which city has surpassed Tokyo as the world's largest city?
mcqs.explanation: Jakarta, Indonesia, with a population of ~42 million, has surpassed Tokyo as the world's largest city.

mcqs.q: In what year did world population first reach 8 billion?
mcqs.explanation: The UN marked 15 November 2022 as the day world population reached 8 billion. It reached 6 billion in 1999 and 7 billion in 2011.

mcqs.q: By what year is world population projected to reach approximately 10 billion?
mcqs.explanation: The UN projects the world population to reach approximately 9.2 billion in 2040, 10 billion in 2060, and 11.2 billion by 2100.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous country in Africa?
mcqs.explanation: Nigeria is Africa's most populous country with about 239 million people.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous country in Europe?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is Europe's most populous country with about 143 million people.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous city in Africa?
mcqs.explanation: Cairo, Egypt, with about 19.4 million people in its metro area, is the most populous city in Africa.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous city in Europe?
mcqs.explanation: Moscow, Russia, with about 14.9 million people, is the most populous city in Europe.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous city in South America?
mcqs.explanation: São Paulo, Brazil, with about 20 million people, is South America's most populous city.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous city in North America?
mcqs.explanation: Mexico City with about 21.8 million people is North America's most populous city.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous city in Oceania?
mcqs.explanation: Sydney, Australia, with about 4.6 million people, is the most populous city in Oceania.

mcqs.q: Which continent has the lowest population density (excl. Antarctica)?
mcqs.explanation: Oceania, with about 5 inhabitants per km², has the lowest population density of any populated continent. Antarctica is even lower at 0.0003.

mcqs.q: Africa's share of world population is approximately:
mcqs.explanation: Africa is the second most populous continent with about 18.83% of world population (~1.55 billion).

mcqs.q: Europe's share of world population is approximately:
mcqs.explanation: Europe makes up about 9.14% of world population (~744 million).

mcqs.q: North America's share of world population is approximately:
mcqs.explanation: North America (primarily USA and Canada) makes up about 4.71% of world population (~388 million).

mcqs.q: Oceania's share of world population is approximately:
mcqs.explanation: Oceania is the least populous region with about 0.57% of world population (~46.6 million).

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous country in North America?
mcqs.explanation: The USA, with about 348 million people, is by far the most populous country in North America.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous country in South America?
mcqs.explanation: Brazil, with about 213 million people, is the most populous country in South America.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous country in Oceania?
mcqs.explanation: Australia, with about 27 million people, is the most populous country in Oceania.

mcqs.q: Which is the most populous country in Asia (as of 2026)?
mcqs.explanation: China remains the most populous country in Asia (~1.47 bn) — though India has overtaken it for the global #1 spot since 2023.

mcqs.q: Which continent has NO permanent human population (only research stations)?
mcqs.explanation: Antarctica has no permanent population — only 3,000-5,000 temporary scientists and staff at research stations like McMurdo (~955 people).

mcqs.q: Which two continents are located entirely in the Southern Hemisphere?
mcqs.explanation: Antarctica and Australia are the only two continents located entirely in the Southern Hemisphere.

mcqs.q: Which continent is crossed by both the Equator and the Prime Meridian?
mcqs.explanation: Africa is the only continent crossed by both the Equator (0° latitude) and the Prime Meridian (0° longitude).

mcqs.q: Which continent is known as the 'Frozen Continent'?
mcqs.explanation: Antarctica is known as the 'Frozen Continent' — about 98% of its surface is covered by ice up to 4 km thick.

mcqs.q: Which continent is sometimes called the 'Dark Continent' due to its long unexplored interior?
mcqs.explanation: Africa was historically called the 'Dark Continent' because its interior remained largely unexplored by Europeans until the 19th century.

mcqs.q: Asia and Europe together form which super-landmass?
mcqs.explanation: Asia and Europe together form Eurasia — the largest contiguous landmass on Earth.

mcqs.q: Africa and Eurasia together form which landmass?
mcqs.explanation: Africa + Eurasia = Afro-Eurasia — the largest land area on Earth (combined Old World).

mcqs.q: Which natural landmark/canal separates the Mediterranean Sea from the Red Sea?
mcqs.explanation: The Suez Canal in Egypt connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, effectively separating Africa from Asia.

mcqs.q: North America and South America are connected by which country?
mcqs.explanation: Panama, via the Isthmus of Panama, connects North and South America. The Panama Canal cuts through it.

mcqs.q: The Ural Mountains and Caucasus form the conventional boundary between which two continents?
mcqs.explanation: The Ural Mountains (Russia) and the Caucasus traditionally mark the boundary between Europe and Asia.

mcqs.q: Which continent is sometimes referred to as the 'New World'?
mcqs.explanation: North and South America are collectively referred to as the 'New World' — a term coined after European contact in the 15th century.

mcqs.q: Which continent does the Sahara Desert occupy?
mcqs.explanation: The Sahara — the world's largest hot desert — covers most of North Africa.

mcqs.q: Which continent contains the world's largest tropical rainforest (Amazon)?
mcqs.explanation: The Amazon Rainforest — the world's largest tropical rainforest — covers most of northern South America (chiefly Brazil).

mcqs.q: Which continent contains the Gobi Desert?
mcqs.explanation: The Gobi Desert is in Asia, spanning northern China and southern Mongolia.

mcqs.q: Which continent does the Nile River flow through?
mcqs.explanation: The Nile flows northward through 11 African countries before reaching the Mediterranean Sea — traditionally considered the world's longest river.

mcqs.q: The Caspian Sea (lowest point in Europe) borders how many countries?
mcqs.explanation: The Caspian Sea is bordered by 5 countries — Russia, Kazakhstan, Turkmenistan, Iran and Azerbaijan.

mcqs.q: Which is the highest active volcano in Antarctica?
mcqs.explanation: Mount Erebus (3,794 m) on Ross Island is Antarctica's highest active volcano — one of the few in the world with a permanent lava lake.

mcqs.q: Which continent has the most countries?
mcqs.explanation: Africa has 54 sovereign countries — the most of any continent. Asia has 49; Europe ~44.

mcqs.q: McMurdo Station — the largest research base in Antarctica — has a population of approximately:
mcqs.explanation: McMurdo Station, operated by the United States, hosts about 955 people during summer — the largest research community in Antarctica.
